settings-singleton-config.name: settings-singleton-config
settings-singleton-config.description: Pydantic Settings-based application configuration loaded once as a module-level singleton, with CSV-fallback env parsing and cross-field validation for CA, DNS, LLM, and key-type consistency.
invariants: MANAGED_DOMAINS is always a List[str], parsed from CSV or JSON
ACME_DIRECTORY_URL is resolved from CA_PROVIDER preset unless CA_PROVIDER='custom'
If LLM_DISABLED=False, langchain must be importable
EAB key ID and HMAC key must both be set or both be empty for digicert/zerossl/sectigo
WEBROOT_PATH required when HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='webroot'
DOMAIN_KEY_SIZE >= 2048 when KEY_TYPE='rsa'
contracts: settings singleton is importable and valid at import time or raises ValueError
callers can replace config.settings for temporary overrides (see main.py, mcp_server.py)

csv-fallback-env-source.name: csv-fallback-env-source
csv-fallback-env-source.description: Custom pydantic-settings sources that catch JSON-parse failures on complex fields and fall back to raw string values so comma-separated env vars can be split by a field_validator.
invariants: Only ValueError from JSON parsing is swallowed; other errors propagate
contracts: List[str]-typed settings fields accept comma-separated strings from env/.env

cli-entrypoint-dispatch.name: cli-entrypoint-dispatch
cli-entrypoint-dispatch.description: Argparse-based CLI that maps mutually-exclusive action flags to handler closures via a command registry, dispatching to run_once/run_revocation/list/status/generate functions.
invariants: Exactly one primary action must be selected or help is printed and process exits 1
First matching command in command_registry is executed; no fallthrough to others
contracts: --expiring-in-30-days and --domain-status never mutate certificate state
--generate-test-cert never contacts ACME servers

settings-override-mechanism.name: settings-override-mechanism
settings-override-mechanism.description: Provides build_settings_from_override and apply_runtime_settings_overrides to construct a derived Settings object (via model_dump + re-instantiation) for one-shot CA provider/directory URL overrides without mutating the base singleton improperly.
invariants: Returns the original settings object unchanged if no overrides given
Overrides never persist beyond process/tool invocation intentionally (module-level swap is explicit)
contracts: Callers can pass settings=... to run_once/run_revocation/list_domains_expiring_within/get_domain_statuses to avoid relying on global singleton

renewal-run-orchestration.name: renewal-run-orchestration
renewal-run-orchestration.description: run_once builds the LangGraph renewal graph and initial state from settings/domains, validates LLM key presence when enabled, and invokes the graph synchronously.
invariants: Exits process (sys.exit(1)) if no managed domains configured
Validates provider API key presence only when LLM_DISABLED=False
contracts: Returns final graph state dict containing completed_renewals/failed_renewals/error_log

revocation-run-orchestration.name: revocation-run-orchestration
revocation-run-orchestration.description: run_revocation validates reason codes and domain list, warns on unmanaged domains, then builds and invokes the revocation graph.
invariants: reason must be one of {0,1,4,5}
domains list must be non-empty
contracts: Returns final state dict with revoked_domains/failed_revocations/error_log

domain-status-and-expiry-queries.name: domain-status-and-expiry-queries
domain-status-and-expiry-queries.description: Read-only query helpers (list_domains_expiring_within, get_domain_statuses) that read certs from filesystem storage and classify status without touching ACME or graph state.
invariants: Never issue/renew/revoke certificates
Sorts expiring domains by ascending days_until_expiry
contracts: Returns empty list / status entries with cert_found=False when cert missing, never raises for missing certs

mcp-tool-surface.name: mcp-tool-surface
mcp-tool-surface.description: FastMCP server exposing ACME lifecycle operations as async tools, each supporting an explicit ca_input_mode ('config' vs 'custom') to resolve CA settings, with a shared asyncio lock serializing state-mutating operations.
invariants: ca_input_mode='custom' requires both ca_provider and acme_directory_url, validated against known choices and http(s) scheme
State-mutating tools (health, renew_once, revoke_cert, generate_test_cert) acquire _MCP_OPERATION_LOCK; read-only tools do not
Settings overrides are applied only for the duration of one tool call via _temporary_settings_override context manager and always restored in finally
contracts: Each tool returns a dict with a 'status' key ('success'/'failed') and never raises to the MCP transport
generate_test_cert only writes under config.settings.CERT_STORE_PATH, never arbitrary paths

mcp-domain-path-traversal-guard.name: mcp-domain-path-traversal-guard
mcp-domain-path-traversal-guard.description: _validate_domain_for_cert_generation rejects domains containing path separators or traversal sequences before using them as a subdirectory name under CERT_STORE_PATH.
invariants: domain must not contain '/', '\\', '..', or start with '.'
domain cannot equal '.' or '..'
contracts: generate_test_cert tool only proceeds with a domain safe for filesystem path construction

mcp-cert-detail-extraction.name: mcp-cert-detail-extraction
mcp-cert-detail-extraction.description: _extract_cert_details parses a PEM certificate with cryptography.x509 to surface subject CN, SANs, issuer org, detected CA, serial, and validity window for the read_cert_details tool.
invariants: Falls back gracefully (None/[]) when CN, issuer org, or SAN extension is absent
contracts: Always returns cert_found=True with either full details or a parse_error entry

run-scoped-logger-singleton.name: run-scoped-logger-singleton
run-scoped-logger-singleton.description: LoggerWithRunID is a process-wide singleton facade wrapping a stdlib logger via LoggerDecorator, injecting a unique run_id into every log line for correlating a single execution across nodes.
invariants: Only one instance ever exists (singleton via __new__ + initialized guard)
run_id is generated once per process and immutable thereafter
contracts: logger.info/error/warning/debug/exception/critical delegate to the same underlying logging.Logger with consistent formatting

renewal-graph-topology.name: renewal-graph-topology
renewal-graph-topology.description: Builds the LangGraph StateGraph for the certificate renewal lifecycle: scan → plan → per-domain ACME order/challenge/finalize/download/store loop, with conditional routing for no-renewals-needed, challenge failure, error handling (retry/skip/abort), and loop continuation.
invariants: renewal_planner always precedes acme_account_setup; no domains are processed without going through pick_next_domain first
error_handler only reachable from challenge_verifier failure path
retry path always passes through retry_scheduler for backoff before looping back to pick_next_domain
All paths eventually reach summary_reporter before END
contracts: build_graph(use_checkpointing=True) returns a graph with MemorySaver attached for resumable runs
initial_state() produces a complete AgentState dict with all required keys initialized

revocation-graph-topology.name: revocation-graph-topology
revocation-graph-topology.description: Separate, simpler StateGraph for certificate revocation: account setup → per-domain pick/revoke loop → reporter, with no error_handler/retry — failures are logged and the loop continues (best-effort revocation).
invariants: max_retries is forced to 0 in revocation_initial_state (retry logic is unused in this graph)
revocation_targets drives the loop exclusively; cert_revoker never blocks the loop on failure
contracts: revocation_initial_state produces a state compatible with the shared AgentState schema, reusing renewal fields as unused placeholders

agent-state-schema.name: agent-state-schema
agent-state-schema.description: TypedDict definitions (AgentState, AcmeOrder, CertRecord) forming the single shared state schema for both renewal and revocation graphs, explicitly excluding secret key material to avoid leaking into trace/checkpoint systems.
invariants: acme_account_key is never stored in state — only account_key_path (a file path) is persisted
AcmeOrder list fields (auth_urls, challenge_urls, challenge_tokens, key_authorizations) are always parallel arrays of equal length
messages field uses add_messages reducer for LangGraph message accumulation
contracts: Both graph builders (graph.py, revocation_graph.py) produce state dicts satisfying this exact schema

node-registry-factory.name: node-registry-factory
node-registry-factory.description: Central NODE_REGISTRY mapping node names to node classes, with get_node() as a factory that instantiates and returns a callable instance for graph.add_node registration, decoupling graph topology from node implementation.
invariants: Every registry value must be a class (type), not an instance; get_node raises TypeError otherwise
acme_account_setup and revocation_account_setup share the same AcmeAccountSetupNode class
contracts: get_node(name) returns a NodeCallable instance implementing __call__(state) -> dict

node-callable-protocol.name: node-callable-protocol
node-callable-protocol.description: Structural typing Protocol defining the minimal contract all graph nodes must satisfy: callable with (state) -> dict.
contracts: Any node class registered in NODE_REGISTRY must implement __call__(self, state: AgentState) -> dict

domain-loop-routing.name: domain-loop-routing
domain-loop-routing.description: PickNextDomainNode pops the next domain from pending_renewals and resets per-domain transient state (retry_count, current_order, current_nonce); accompanying router functions (domain_loop_router, challenge_router, renewal_router, error_action_router) provide conditional-edge decisions for the renewal graph.
invariants: Each call to PickNextDomainNode resets retry_count to 0 and retry_delay_seconds to 5 for the new domain
error_action_router falls back to 'skip' action if error_analysis JSON is unparseable
retry action is only honored if retry_count < max_retries, otherwise routed as skip
contracts: domain_loop_router returns 'next_domain' iff pending_renewals is non-empty

ca-mismatch-detection-scoped-to-custom.name: ca-mismatch-detection-scoped-to-custom
ca-mismatch-detection-scoped-to-custom.description: certificate_scanner reads existing cert files per managed domain and computes expiry/renewal status; CA-provider mismatch detection via issuer inspection is only performed when CA_PROVIDER='custom', since named providers are treated as authoritative.
invariants: detected_ca_provider is None whenever CA_PROVIDER != 'custom'
needs_renewal is True whenever no cert is found or days_until_expiry < threshold
letsencrypt and letsencrypt_staging are treated as equivalent for mismatch warnings
contracts: Returns one CertRecord per managed domain, in the same order as managed_domains

planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch.name: planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch
planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch.description: RenewalPlannerNode dispatches between an LLM-based classification path (urgent/routine/skip via JSON prompt) and a deterministic fallback (no-cert domains first, then expiry-ascending) based on config.settings.LLM_DISABLED, with output validation stripping hallucinated domains not in managed_domains.
invariants: Every managed domain must end up classified into exactly one bucket after _parse_and_validate (missing domains default to routine)
pending_renewals order is always urgent-then-routine regardless of path taken
Deterministic order is strictly [no_cert_domains] + [expiring_soon sorted by (days, expiry_date) ascending]
contracts: Always returns renewal_plan (str) and pending_renewals (list[str]) keys
LLM path never allows a domain outside managed_domains into pending_renewals

acme-account-key-secure-sidechannel.name: acme-account-key-secure-sidechannel
acme-account-key-secure-sidechannel.description: AcmeAccountSetupNode ensures an ACME account exists by attempting lookup-then-register flow (RFC 8555 §7.3/7.3.1), loading/generating the account private key from/to disk rather than passing it through AgentState.
invariants: Account key material is never placed into the returned state dict
A new account key is only generated if account_key_path does not already contain one
If account key exists but lookup returns no account_url, falls through to re-registration
contracts: Returns acme_account_url and current_nonce on success

order-initializer-multi-san-and-dns01.name: order-initializer-multi-san-and-dns01
order-initializer-multi-san-and-dns01.description: OrderInitializerNode creates an ACME order and fetches per-authorization challenge data, branching between http-01 and dns-01 challenge types based on config.settings.HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE, computing DNS TXT values only in dns mode.
invariants: auth_urls, challenge_urls, challenge_tokens, key_authorizations, auth_domains are parallel lists of equal length
dns_txt_values is populated only when HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='dns', otherwise empty
Missing challenge of the expected type for any authorization aborts order creation with an error_log entry
contracts: Returns a fully populated AcmeOrder in current_order on success, or partial state + error_log on missing-challenge failure

challenge-setup-verify-lifecycle.name: challenge-setup-verify-lifecycle
challenge-setup-verify-lifecycle.description: ChallengeSetupNode/ChallengeVerifierNode manage the lifecycle of standalone HTTP server, webroot files, or DNS TXT records across setup, per-authorization verification with token-swapping for multi-SAN standalone mode, and always-cleanup via _cleanup_challenge.
invariants: Module-level _standalone_server/_dns_provider persist across setup and verify calls within a domain's processing
Already-valid authorizations are skipped (not re-responded-to) to avoid ACME 'malformed' errors
_cleanup_challenge is invoked both on success and on any per-authorization failure
Standalone mode serves only one token at a time, swapping between authorizations sequentially
contracts: challenge_verifier returns current_order with status 'ready' on full success or 'invalid' with error_log entry on failure

csr-generation-key-material-isolation.name: csr-generation-key-material-isolation
csr-generation-key-material-isolation.description: CsrGeneratorNode generates a domain key (RSA or ECC per config), writes privkey.pem atomically with 0o600 permissions to the cert store, and stores only the DER CSR (hex-encoded) in state — never the private key.
invariants: Private key file permissions are always restricted to owner read/write (0o600)
domain private key object itself never enters AgentState; only csr_der_hex does
contracts: current_order.csr_der_hex is valid hex-encoded DER CSR for the current_domain after this node runs

finalize-and-download-poll-loop.name: finalize-and-download-poll-loop
finalize-and-download-poll-loop.description: OrderFinalizerNode submits the CSR and polls until the order is valid; CertDownloaderNode performs a replay-safe POST-as-GET download of the full certificate chain PEM.
invariants: Finalization requires csr_der_hex already present in current_order (from csr_generator)
Certificate URL download is idempotent/replay-safe per RFC 8555 §7.4.2
contracts: On failure, current_order.status is set to 'invalid' and error_log is appended, never raises to caller

storage-manager-pem-chain-split.name: storage-manager-pem-chain-split
storage-manager-pem-chain-split.description: StorageManagerNode splits the downloaded full PEM chain into leaf cert.pem and chain.pem, verifies the privkey.pem written earlier by csr_generator exists, and writes all files plus metadata.json atomically via storage.filesystem.
invariants: Assumes DigiCert-style chain ordering: first PEM block is leaf, remaining blocks are intermediates
Fails (adds to failed_renewals) rather than raising if privkey.pem is missing or full_chain_pem is empty
contracts: On success, appends domain to completed_renewals and updates cert_metadata[domain]

error-handler-deterministic-decision.name: error-handler-deterministic-decision
error-handler-deterministic-decision.description: ErrorHandlerNode deterministically classifies the last error as fatal (immediate abort of entire run, draining pending_renewals into failed_renewals) or retryable (exponential backoff capped at 300s, up to max_retries, then skip).
invariants: Fatal error patterns (unauthorized, accountdoesnotexist, badkey, externalaccountrequired) always trigger abort regardless of retry_count
Abort clears pending_renewals entirely and marks them all failed
Backoff delay = min(retry_delay_seconds * 2^(retry_count+1), 300)
contracts: Always returns error_analysis (str) describing the decision for downstream error_action_router

retry-backoff-scheduler.name: retry-backoff-scheduler
retry-backoff-scheduler.description: RetrySchedulerNode blocks synchronously (time.sleep) until retry_not_before timestamp elapses, decoupling the timing concern from error_handler's decision logic; an async variant exists for future non-blocking use but is not wired into the graph.
invariants: retry_not_before is always cleared to None after this node runs
No-op (returns {}) when retry_not_before is None
contracts: Downstream pick_next_domain is only reached after the scheduled backoff period has elapsed

deterministic-summary-reporting.name: deterministic-summary-reporting
deterministic-summary-reporting.description: SummaryReporterNode and RevocationReporterNode produce deterministic, formatted text summaries (SUCCESS/PARTIAL/FAILED) of a renewal or revocation run without any LLM involvement, printing to stdout and logging.
invariants: Status is FAILED only if there are zero completed/revoked items and at least one failure; PARTIAL if both present; SUCCESS if no failures
Skipped domains (renewal) = managed_domains minus (completed ∪ failed)
contracts: Always prints a summary and returns {'messages': []}, never raises

revocation-domain-loop-routing.name: revocation-domain-loop-routing
revocation-domain-loop-routing.description: PickNextRevocationDomainNode pops the next domain from revocation_targets and clears current_nonce to force a fresh nonce fetch; revocation_loop_router routes based on remaining targets.
invariants: current_nonce is always reset to None when picking the next revocation domain
contracts: revocation_loop_router returns 'next_domain' iff revocation_targets is non-empty

cert-revocation-best-effort.name: cert-revocation-best-effort
cert-revocation-best-effort.description: CertRevokerNode revokes a single domain's certificate via ACME POST /revokeCert, never raising on failure — errors are captured into failed_revocations and error_log so the revocation loop can continue to the next domain.
invariants: current_revocation_domain is always cleared to None after processing (success or failure)
Reason code 0 is handled distinctly (omitted from ACME payload per RFC 8555 §7.6, delegated to acme.client)
contracts: Never propagates AcmeError to the graph; always returns a dict with either revoked_domains or failed_revocations updated

llm-optional-factory.name: llm-optional-factory
llm-optional-factory.description: make_llm() centralizes chat-model construction across anthropic/openai/ollama providers via langchain's init_chat_model, only importable/usable when LLM_DISABLED=False and langchain extras are installed; raises clear ImportError/ValueError otherwise.
invariants: This module is only imported when LLM_DISABLED=False (by caller convention, e.g. planner.py)
provider must be one of anthropic/openai/ollama or ValueError is raised
Required API key must be set for anthropic/openai providers before invocation
contracts: make_llm(model, max_tokens) returns a BaseChatModel instance ready for .invoke()

llm-prompt-templates.name: llm-prompt-templates
llm-prompt-templates.description: Plain-string prompt templates for the three LLM-capable nodes (planner, error_handler, summary/revocation reporter), each enforcing strict JSON-only or plain-text-only output contracts to keep downstream parsing reliable.
invariants: PLANNER prompts require valid JSON with no prose/markdown fences
ERROR_HANDLER prompt requires exactly one of retry/skip/abort as action
contracts: Callers (planner.py, error_handler equivalents) can safely json.loads() the LLM response content when following these templates
